content.text:
Design an Auction System.

Items are listed for auction and bidders place bids. The system must always track the current HIGHEST bid for each auction, using OPTIMISTIC CONCURRENCY (a compare-and-set / CAS retry loop) so that a stale or lower bid is rejected and there is never a lost update when many bidders bid at the same instant.

Core Requirements:
- Create / start / close auctions; an auction has a lifecycle State: SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE -> CLOSED.
- place_bid(auction_id, bidder, amount): accepted only if it clears the current highest by a minimum increment (or meets the starting price for the first bid). Returns the accepted bid; rejects too-low bids and bids on a closed auction with typed errors.
- Highest-bid tracking via an atomic compare-and-set cell with a retry loop: under concurrency the highest bid moves monotonically up, with no lost update and exactly one winner.
- Soft-close auto-extend: a bid within the last N seconds pushes the end time out.
- Notify outbid bidders (Observer).

Design Considerations:
- Patterns: optimistic CAS on the highest bid, State (auction lifecycle), Observer (notifications), Strategy (pluggable min-increment policy).
- Use a per-auction lock OR a true CAS loop on an atomic highest-bid cell вАФ explain the choice.
- Prove correctness with a concurrency test: N bidders bid concurrently via a threading.Barrier; assert the final highest is the maximum valid bid, monotonic (no lost update), and exactly one winner; plus soft-close, bid-below-current, and bid-after-close tests.
Let me build and verify the design before presenting it.

# Python 3.11+
"""Domain model for the Auction System.

Like a thread-pool problem, concurrency IS the domain here: many bidders race to
raise the SAME highest-bid cell. So instead of pretending there's a "clean
single-threaded core" and bolting a lock on later, the hard part lives in ONE
tiny primitive вАФ an `AtomicCell` that does compare-and-set (CAS). The auction
then raises its highest bid with an optimistic retry loop: read the current
highest, validate, and swap it in ONLY if nobody beat us to it. A stale or lower
bid loses the swap, re-reads, and is rejected вАФ so there is never a lost update.

рЯОЩпЄП "The whole trick is one compare-and-set cell. A bid is accepted only if the
highest bid is still exactly what I read a moment ago. If someone slipped a
higher bid in between, my swap fails, I re-read, and now my bid is too low вАФ so
it's cleanly rejected instead of silently overwriting the real winner."
"""

import threading
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import Generic, TYPE_CHECKING, TypeVar

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    # Type-only import: avoids a models <-> strategies import cycle at runtime.
    from strategies import BidValidationStrategy

T = TypeVar("T")


# вФАвФА VOCABULARY вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
class AuctionStatus(Enum):
    SCHEDULED = "SCHEDULED"  # created, not yet open for bids
    ACTIVE = "ACTIVE"        # open вАФ bids accepted
    CLOSED = "CLOSED"        # ended вАФ winner frozen, no more bids


class EventKind(Enum):
    NEW_HIGHEST = "NEW_HIGHEST"  # a bid became the new top bid
    OUTBID = "OUTBID"            # a bidder who was on top got displaced
    CLOSED = "CLOSED"            # the auction ended; carries the winner


# вФАвФА EXCEPTIONS (one base, one subclass per failure mode) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
class AuctionError(Exception):
    """Base for every error this system raises вАФ callers catch one type."""


class UnknownAuctionError(AuctionError):
    """No auction exists for the given id."""


class InvalidAuctionError(AuctionError):
    """create_auction got a non-positive price/duration or a bad window."""


class AuctionNotActiveError(AuctionError):
    """A bid (or close) arrived while the auction was still SCHEDULED."""


class AuctionClosedError(AuctionError):
    """A bid arrived after the auction CLOSED or after its end time passed."""


class BidTooLowError(AuctionError):
    """The bid did not clear the current highest plus the minimum increment."""


# вФАвФА VALUE OBJECTS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Bidder:
    """A participant who places bids. A value object вАФ the system never mutates it.

    рЯОЩпЄП "A Bidder is just an id and a name. They CALL the system; we don't store
    their state вАФ we only remember WHICH bidder owns the current highest bid."
    """
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Bid:
    """An immutable snapshot of one bid on the highest-bid cell.

    `version` is the CAS version: 0 is the opening "floor" (no real bid yet);
    every accepted bid increments it by exactly one. Because each accepted bid is
    a brand-new object, comparing snapshots by identity is a perfect version
    check вАФ the same value can never be reused, so there is no ABA problem.
    """
    bidder_id: str
    amount: int
    version: int

    @property
    def is_floor(self) -> bool:
        # WHY: version 0 means "starting price, nobody has actually bid yet".
        return self.version == 0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AuctionState:
    """The single atomically-swapped snapshot: the highest bid AND the end time.

    Bundling these two together is the key move вАФ a soft-close extension and the
    new highest bid are installed in ONE compare-and-set, so they can never drift
    apart (you can't end up with the new bid but the old deadline, or vice versa).
    """
    highest: Bid
    ends_at: float


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BidOutcome:
    """What `Auction.place_bid` returns after a successful swap."""
    winning_bid: Bid
    previous_bid: Bid | None  # the displaced top bidder (None if it beat the floor)
    extended: bool            # did this bid push the end time out (soft close)?
    ends_at: float


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AuctionEvent:
    """A notification handed to observers. `bidder_id` is the RECIPIENT."""
    kind: EventKind
    auction_id: str
    bidder_id: str
    amount: int
    extended: bool = False
    ends_at: float = 0.0


# вФАвФА ATOMIC CELL: the compare-and-set primitive вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
class AtomicCell(Generic[T]):
    # вФАвФА WHY THIS CLASS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
    # A one-slot register that supports compare-and-set, like Java's
    # AtomicReference. The whole concurrency story rests on this: `get()` reads a
    # snapshot, `compare_and_set(expected, new)` swaps ONLY if the value is still
    # exactly the snapshot we read. The lock is held for nanoseconds вАФ just long
    # enough to make the read-check-write one indivisible step.
    # рЯОЩпЄП "This is a software stand-in for a hardware CAS instruction: update the
    #     value only if it hasn't changed since I looked. That one guarantee is
    #     what makes the lock-free bid loop correct."
    def __init__(self, initial: T) -> None:
        self._value = initial
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def get(self) -> T:
        with self._lock:
            return self._value

    def compare_and_set(self, expected: T, new: T) -> bool:
        # WHY: identity check (`is`), not equality. Each accepted bid is a NEW
        #      object, so "still the same object I read" means "nobody swapped in
        #      between". That defeats the ABA problem by construction.
        with self._lock:
            if self._value is expected:
                self._value = new
                return True
            return False


# вФАвФА OBSERVER: bidder notifications (decoupled from the bid path) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
class AuctionObserver(ABC):
    # вФАвФА WHY THIS CLASS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
    # The seam that decouples "a bid happened" from "tell people about it". The
    # service fires events AFTER a bid commits, never while swapping the cell, so
    # a slow notifier can never block a bidder.
    @abstractmethod
    def notify(self, event: AuctionEvent) -> None:
        """Handle one auction event (new highest / outbid / closed)."""
        raise NotImplementedError


class ConsoleNotifier(AuctionObserver):
    """Prints a human-friendly line per event вАФ used by the demo driver."""

    def notify(self, event: AuctionEvent) -> None:
        if event.kind is EventKind.OUTBID:
            print(f"   notify -> {event.bidder_id}: you were OUTBID (top is now {event.amount})")
        elif event.kind is EventKind.NEW_HIGHEST:
            tail = f" [end extended to {event.ends_at:.0f}]" if event.extended else ""
            print(f"   notify -> {event.bidder_id} is the new highest bidder at {event.amount}{tail}")
        else:
            print(f"   notify -> auction {event.auction_id} CLOSED; winner {event.bidder_id} at {event.amount}")


class RecordingObserver(AuctionObserver):
    """Stores every event in a list so tests (and the demo) can assert on them."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.events: list[AuctionEvent] = []

    def notify(self, event: AuctionEvent) -> None:
        self.events.append(event)


# вФАвФА AUCTION: the lifecycle state machine + the lock-free bid loop вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
class Auction:
    # вФАвФА WHY THIS CLASS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
    # The heart of the system. It owns three things and guards their rules:
    #   1. its lifecycle (SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE -> CLOSED) вАФ illegal jumps are
    #      impossible because only this class flips `status`,
    #   2. its highest-bid cell вАФ raised ONLY through a compare-and-set loop, so
    #      two bidders can never both "win" the same slot (no lost update),
    #   3. its end time вАФ extended in the SAME swap as the bid (soft close), so
    #      the deadline and the winning bid can never drift apart.
    # рЯОЩпЄП "Only the auction can change its own status or its own highest bid. The
    #     orchestrator never reaches in and writes them вАФ it asks the auction, and
    #     the auction enforces every rule itself."
    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str,
        item_name: str,
        starting_price: int,
        duration: float,
        soft_close_window: float,
        extension: float,
        policy: "BidValidationStrategy",
        now_fn: Callable[[], float],
    ) -> None:
        self.id = id
        self.item_name = item_name
        self.starting_price = starting_price
        self.status = AuctionStatus.SCHEDULED
        self._duration = duration
        self._soft_close_window = soft_close_window
        self._extension = extension
        self._policy = policy
        self._now = now_fn
        # Version 0 is the floor: "the price to beat is the starting price".
        floor = Bid(bidder_id="", amount=starting_price, version=0)
        self._cell: AtomicCell[AuctionState] = AtomicCell(AuctionState(highest=floor, ends_at=0.0))
        # A short mutex for the RARE control-plane transitions (start/close) вАФ the
        # contended hot path (place_bid) stays lock-free via the CAS cell.
        self._lifecycle_lock = threading.Lock()

    # вФАвФА lifecycle transitions вФАвФА
    def start(self) -> None:
        with self._lifecycle_lock:
            if self.status is not AuctionStatus.SCHEDULED:
                raise AuctionNotActiveError(
                    f"auction {self.id} cannot start from {self.status.value}"
                )
            now = self._now()
            snapshot = self._cell.get()
            # WHY: install the real deadline BEFORE opening, so the first bidder
            #      to see ACTIVE already reads a correct end time.
            self._cell.compare_and_set(
                snapshot, AuctionState(snapshot.highest, now + self._duration)
            )
            self.status = AuctionStatus.ACTIVE

    def close(self) -> Bid | None:
        with self._lifecycle_lock:
            if self.status is AuctionStatus.CLOSED:
                return self.highest_bid()            # idempotent: second close is a no-op
            if self.status is AuctionStatus.SCHEDULED:
                raise AuctionNotActiveError(f"auction {self.id} was never started")
            self.status = AuctionStatus.CLOSED
            return self.highest_bid()

    # вФАвФА the optimistic compare-and-set bid loop вФАвФА
    def place_bid(self, bidder_id: str, amount: int) -> BidOutcome:
        # WHY: a SCHEDULED auction has no deadline yet вАФ reject before reading it.
        if self.status is AuctionStatus.SCHEDULED:
            raise AuctionNotActiveError(f"auction {self.id} has not started")

        while True:
            state = self._cell.get()                 # optimistic read of the highest bid
            now = self._now()

            # Re-checked every retry: the deadline can pass, or close() can fire,
            # while we loop вАФ a stale bid must not sneak in after the end.
            if self.status is AuctionStatus.CLOSED or now >= state.ends_at:
                raise AuctionClosedError(f"auction {self.id} is closed")

            floor = self._policy.min_acceptable(state.highest, self.starting_price)
            if amount < floor:
                raise BidTooLowError(
                    f"bid {amount} is below the minimum {floor} for auction {self.id}"
                )

            new_highest = Bid(bidder_id, amount, version=state.highest.version + 1)
            new_ends_at = self._extended_end(state.ends_at, now)
            candidate = AuctionState(new_highest, new_ends_at)

            # The ONE atomic step: swap in our bid ONLY if the highest is still
            # exactly what we read. If someone bid in between, this fails and we
            # loop вАФ on the retry our amount is now likely below the new floor, so
            # it is cleanly rejected instead of overwriting the real winner.
            if self._cell.compare_and_set(state, candidate):
                previous = None if state.highest.is_floor else state.highest
                return BidOutcome(new_highest, previous, new_ends_at > state.ends_at, new_ends_at)

    # вФАвФА queries вФАвФА
    def highest_bid(self) -> Bid | None:
        highest = self._cell.get().highest
        return None if highest.is_floor else highest

    def current_ends_at(self) -> float:
        return self._cell.get().ends_at

    # вФАвФА helper вФАвФА
    def _extended_end(self, ends_at: float, now: float) -> float:
        # WHY: a bid landing within the soft-close window pushes the end out so a
        #      sniper can't win in the final second вАФ everyone gets to respond.
        if ends_at - now <= self._soft_close_window:
            return now + self._extension
        return ends_at

# Python 3.11+
"""Bid-validation strategies вАФ the seam that decides the MINIMUM a new bid must
clear to beat the current highest.

рЯОЩпЄП "The auction doesn't hardcode 'you must beat the top by at least 1'. It asks
a policy. Today it's a flat +1 increment; tomorrow the business wants '+5% of the
current price' or 'no minimum'. Each is a new class, zero auction changes вАФ
that's the Strategy pattern earning its keep."
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from math import ceil

from models import Bid, InvalidAuctionError


class BidValidationStrategy(ABC):
    # вФАвФА WHY THIS CLASS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
    # The abstraction the Auction depends on (Dependency Inversion). Given the
    # current highest bid (or the floor), it returns the smallest amount a NEW
    # bid must meet. The auction never knows which concrete rule is wired in.
    @abstractmethod
    def min_acceptable(self, current: Bid, starting_price: int) -> int:
        """Return the minimum amount the next bid must meet to be accepted."""
        raise NotImplementedError


class FixedIncrementPolicy(BidValidationStrategy):
    """Flat step: the next bid must clear the top by at least `increment`."""

    def __init__(self, increment: int = 1) -> None:
        if increment < 1:
            # A non-positive step would let equal bids tie and break "one winner".
            raise InvalidAuctionError("increment must be >= 1")
        self._increment = increment

    def min_acceptable(self, current: Bid, starting_price: int) -> int:
        # WHY: the very first bid only has to MEET the starting price; after that
        #      every bid must beat the standing top by the fixed step.
        if current.is_floor:
            return starting_price
        return current.amount + self._increment


class PercentIncrementPolicy(BidValidationStrategy):
    """Proportional step: the next bid must clear the top by at least `pct`%.

    Used by high-value auctions where a flat +1 is meaningless against a 1M bid.
    """

    def __init__(self, pct: float) -> None:
        if pct <= 0:
            raise InvalidAuctionError("pct must be > 0")
        self._pct = pct

    def min_acceptable(self, current: Bid, starting_price: int) -> int:
        if current.is_floor:
            return starting_price
        # WHY: ceil so a fractional step always rounds UP to a strictly higher
        #      integer вАФ otherwise a tiny percentage could round to +0 and tie.
        return current.amount + ceil(current.amount * self._pct)

# Python 3.11+
"""AuctionService вАФ the orchestrator (the system's public API).

It owns the registry of auctions and the per-auction observer lists, mints ids,
and injects the clock. It never reaches into an auction's highest-bid cell or its
status directly вАФ it asks the `Auction`, which enforces every rule itself. After a
bid commits, the service fires observer notifications OUTSIDE any critical section,
so a slow notifier can never block a bidder.
"""

import itertools
import time
from collections.abc import Callable

from models import (
    Auction,
    AuctionEvent,
    AuctionObserver,
    Bid,
    Bidder,
    BidTooLowError,
    EventKind,
    InvalidAuctionError,
    UnknownAuctionError,
)
from strategies import BidValidationStrategy, FixedIncrementPolicy


class AuctionService:
    # вФАвФА WHY THIS CLASS вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
    # The single entry point. Callers only ever touch these six methods; the
    # auction lifecycle, the lock-free bid loop, and the soft-close logic all live
    # behind them. The clock is injected so expiry/soft-close are testable without
    # real waiting.
    def __init__(self, now_fn: Callable[[], float] = time.monotonic) -> None:
        self._now = now_fn
        self._auctions: dict[str, Auction] = {}
        self._observers: dict[str, list[AuctionObserver]] = {}
        self._ids = itertools.count(1)

    # вФАвФА create / lifecycle вФАвФА
    def create_auction(
        self,
        item_name: str,
        starting_price: int,
        duration: float,
        min_increment: int = 1,
        soft_close_window: float = 0.0,
        extension: float = 0.0,
        policy: BidValidationStrategy | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        """Register a new auction in SCHEDULED state; return its id."""
        if starting_price <= 0:
            raise InvalidAuctionError("starting_price must be > 0")
        if duration <= 0:
            raise InvalidAuctionError("duration must be > 0")
        if soft_close_window < 0 or extension < 0:
            raise InvalidAuctionError("soft_close_window and extension must be >= 0")

        chosen = policy or FixedIncrementPolicy(min_increment)
        auction_id = f"auc-{next(self._ids)}"
        self._auctions[auction_id] = Auction(
            id=auction_id,
            item_name=item_name,
            starting_price=starting_price,
            duration=duration,
            soft_close_window=soft_close_window,
            extension=extension,
            policy=chosen,
            now_fn=self._now,
        )
        self._observers[auction_id] = []
        return auction_id

    def start(self, auction_id: str) -> None:
        """Open an auction for bids (SCHEDULED -> ACTIVE)."""
        self._require(auction_id).start()

    def close(self, auction_id: str) -> Bid | None:
        """End an auction (ACTIVE -> CLOSED); return the winning bid or None."""
        winner = self._require(auction_id).close()
        if winner is not None:
            self._emit(auction_id, AuctionEvent(
                EventKind.CLOSED, auction_id, winner.bidder_id, winner.amount,
            ))
        return winner

    # вФАвФА the bid pipeline вФАвФА
    def place_bid(self, auction_id: str, bidder: Bidder, amount: int) -> Bid:
        """Place a bid; on success return the accepted Bid, else raise a typed error."""
        if amount <= 0:
            raise BidTooLowError("bid amount must be a positive integer")
        auction = self._require(auction_id)

        # The auction runs the optimistic CAS loop and raises on closed/too-low.
        outcome = auction.place_bid(bidder.id, amount)

        # Notify AFTER the bid commits вАФ never while swapping the cell.
        if outcome.previous_bid is not None and outcome.previous_bid.bidder_id != bidder.id:
            self._emit(auction_id, AuctionEvent(
                EventKind.OUTBID, auction_id,
                outcome.previous_bid.bidder_id, outcome.winning_bid.amount,
            ))
        self._emit(auction_id, AuctionEvent(
            EventKind.NEW_HIGHEST, auction_id, bidder.id, outcome.winning_bid.amount,
            extended=outcome.extended, ends_at=outcome.ends_at,
        ))
        return outcome.winning_bid

    # вФАвФА queries / observers вФАвФА
    def highest_bid(self, auction_id: str) -> Bid | None:
        """The current highest real bid, or None if nobody has bid yet."""
        return self._require(auction_id).highest_bid()

    def subscribe(self, auction_id: str, observer: AuctionObserver) -> None:
        """Register an observer to receive this auction's events."""
        self._require(auction_id)  # validate the id exists before subscribing
        self._observers[auction_id].append(observer)

    # вФАвФА private helpers вФАвФА
    def _require(self, auction_id: str) -> Auction:
        auction = self._auctions.get(auction_id)
        if auction is None:
            raise UnknownAuctionError(f"no auction with id {auction_id!r}")
        return auction

    def _emit(self, auction_id: str, event: AuctionEvent) -> None:
        # WHY: a throwing or slow observer must not break bid delivery or take
        #      down the bidder's thread вАФ isolate each callback.
        for observer in self._observers.get(auction_id, []):
            try:
                observer.notify(event)
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - observers must not break the bid path
                pass

# Python 3.11+
"""Narrated demo driver for the Auction System.

Run: `python3 main.py`. Each case prints a one-line "Case N ... -> OK".
The clock is injected (a tiny dict) so the soft-close extension is deterministic
and the printed deadlines are clean вАФ no real waiting.
"""

import random
import threading

from auction_service import AuctionService
from models import (
    AuctionClosedError,
    BidTooLowError,
    Bidder,
    ConsoleNotifier,
    EventKind,
    RecordingObserver,
)


def main() -> None:
    clock = {"t": 1000.0}
    svc = AuctionService(now_fn=lambda: clock["t"])

    alice = Bidder("u-alice", "Alice")
    bob = Bidder("u-bob", "Bob")
    carol = Bidder("u-carol", "Carol")

    # вФАвФА Case 1: a bidding war, with outbid notifications вФАвФА
    aid = svc.create_auction(
        "Vintage Guitar", starting_price=100, duration=60,
        min_increment=10, soft_close_window=10, extension=15,
    )
    svc.subscribe(aid, ConsoleNotifier())
    rec = RecordingObserver()
    svc.subscribe(aid, rec)
    svc.start(aid)

    print("Case 1: bidding war on 'Vintage Guitar' (start 100, +10 min increment)")
    svc.place_bid(aid, alice, 100)   # beats the floor
    svc.place_bid(aid, bob, 150)     # outbids Alice
    svc.place_bid(aid, carol, 200)   # outbids Bob
    svc.place_bid(aid, alice, 250)   # outbids Carol
    top = svc.highest_bid(aid)
    print(f"   highest bid is now {top.amount} by {top.bidder_id}")
    outbids = [e for e in rec.events if e.kind is EventKind.OUTBID]
    assert top.amount == 250 and top.bidder_id == alice.id
    assert len(outbids) == 3, outbids
    print("Case 1: each higher bid displaces the last; 3 outbid notices fired -> OK")

    # вФАвФА Case 2: a late bid pushes the end time out (soft close) вФАвФА
    print("Case 2: soft-close auto-extend")
    clock["t"] = 1055.0              # 5s before the 1060 deadline -> inside the 10s window
    svc.place_bid(aid, bob, 300)
    last = rec.events[-1]
    print(f"   bid at t=1055 (deadline was 1060) -> extended={last.extended}, new deadline={last.ends_at:.0f}")
    assert last.extended is True and last.ends_at == 1070.0
    print("Case 2: a bid inside the final window extended the deadline -> OK")

    # вФАвФА Case 3: a bid that doesn't clear the increment is rejected вФАвФА
    print("Case 3: a bid below current highest + increment is rejected")
    try:
        svc.place_bid(aid, carol, 305)   # top is 300, min is 310
        assert False, "should have raised BidTooLowError"
    except BidTooLowError as e:
        print(f"   bid 305 vs minimum 310 -> {e}")
    print("Case 3: stale / low bid rejected, no lost update -> OK")

    # вФАвФА Case 4: after close, all bids are rejected вФАвФА
    print("Case 4: bids after close are rejected")
    winner = svc.close(aid)
    print(f"   auction closed; winner {winner.bidder_id} at {winner.amount}")
    assert winner.amount == 300 and winner.bidder_id == bob.id
    try:
        svc.place_bid(aid, alice, 1000)
        assert False, "should have raised AuctionClosedError"
    except AuctionClosedError as e:
        print(f"   late bid 1000 -> {e}")
    print("Case 4: bid after close rejected -> OK")

    # вФАвФА Case 5: 50 bidders race at once -> exactly one winner, no lost update вФАвФА
    print("Case 5: 50 bidders bid concurrently on 'Rare Coin'")
    clock["t"] = 2000.0
    aid2 = svc.create_auction("Rare Coin", starting_price=1, duration=10_000, min_increment=1)
    svc.start(aid2)
    n = 50
    amounts = list(range(1, n + 1))
    random.shuffle(amounts)
    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)
    accepted = []
    lock = threading.Lock()

    def bidder_thread(i: int) -> None:
        person = Bidder(f"u-{i}", f"bidder-{i}")
        amount = amounts[i]
        barrier.wait()               # all 50 hit place_bid at the same instant
        try:
            bid = svc.place_bid(aid2, person, amount)
            with lock:
                accepted.append(bid)
        except BidTooLowError:
            pass                     # lost the CAS race AND now below the new floor

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=bidder_thread, args=(i,)) for i in range(n)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    final = svc.highest_bid(aid2)
    versions = sorted(b.version for b in accepted)
    print(f"   {len(accepted)} bids accepted, final highest = {final.amount} (max possible = {n})")
    assert final.amount == n
    assert versions == list(range(1, len(accepted) + 1))   # one CAS per accept, no gaps
    print("Case 5: final highest = max valid bid; versions contiguous (no lost update) -> OK")

    print("\nAll cases passed.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    assert (EventKind.OUTBID, "u1") in kinds

    assert bid.amount == 100


# вФАвФА soft-close auto-extend вФАвФА
def test_soft_close_extends_on_late_bid(svc, clock):
    aid = svc.create_auction("x", 100, duration=60, soft_close_window=10, extension=15)
    svc.start(aid)                                    # ends at 1060
    auction = svc._auctions[aid]
    clock["t"] = 1055.0                               # 5s left -> inside the window
    svc.place_bid(aid, Bidder("u1", "A"), 100)
    assert auction.current_ends_at() == 1070.0        # 1055 + 15


def test_soft_close_does_not_extend_outside_window(svc, clock):
    aid = svc.create_auction("x", 100, duration=60, soft_close_window=10, extension=15)
    svc.start(aid)                                    # ends at 1060
    auction = svc._auctions[aid]
    clock["t"] = 1020.0                               # 40s left -> outside the window
    svc.place_bid(aid, Bidder("u1", "A"), 100)
    assert auction.current_ends_at() == 1060.0        # unchanged


# вФАвФА MANDATORY concurrency proof (Barrier => everyone races at one instant) вФАвФА
def test_concurrent_bids_no_lost_update_single_winner(svc):
    """N bidders bid distinct amounts at the same instant. We assert:
      1) the FINAL highest is the maximum valid bid,
      2) exactly ONE winner holds it,
      3) the highest moved monotonically up вАФ one CAS per accept, contiguous
         versions, strictly increasing amounts (no lost update),
      4) every rejection is the typed BidTooLowError and every bidder got an outcome.
    """
    aid = svc.create_auction("coin", 1, duration=10_000, min_increment=1)
    svc.start(aid)

    n = 64
    amounts = list(range(1, n + 1))                   # distinct, max is n
    random.shuffle(amounts)
    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)
    accepted: list[Bid] = []
    rejected: list[BidTooLowError] = []
    lock = threading.Lock()

    def attempt(i: int) -> None:
        person = Bidder(f"u-{i}", f"b{i}")
        amount = amounts[i]
        barrier.wait()                                # all n hit place_bid together
        try:
            bid = svc.place_bid(aid, person, amount)
            with lock:
                accepted.append(bid)
        except BidTooLowError as exc:                 # lost the CAS race, now below floor
            with lock:
                rejected.append(exc)

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(i,)) for i in range(n)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    final = svc.highest_bid(aid)

    # 1) the final highest IS the maximum valid bid
    assert final.amount == n
    # 2) exactly one winner holds the final amount, and it's the bidder who bid n
    assert sum(1 for b in accepted if b.amount == final.amount) == 1
    assert final.bidder_id == f"u-{amounts.index(n)}"
    # 3) monotonic / no lost update: one CAS per accept, contiguous versions, strictly up
    by_version = sorted(accepted, key=lambda b: b.version)
    assert [b.version for b in by_version] == list(range(1, len(accepted) + 1))
    amts = [b.amount for b in by_version]
    assert all(amts[k] < amts[k + 1] for k in range(len(amts) - 1))
    # 4) every bidder got exactly one outcome and every loss is the typed error
    assert len(accepted) + len(rejected) == n
    assert all(isinstance(e, BidTooLowError) for e in rejected)

All tests pass. Here's the complete, interview-ready design.

> рЯОЩпЄП **Open with this framing** (it's the senior move for this problem): "An auction's hard part isn't the entities вАФ it's that every bidder races to raise the *same* highest-bid cell. So I won't pretend there's a 'clean single-threaded core' and bolt a lock on at the end. I'll make the bid update an **optimistic compare-and-set loop** on one atomic cell: read the current top, validate, and swap my bid in *only if nobody beat me to it*. A stale or lower bid loses the swap and is cleanly rejected вАФ there's never a lost update, and there's always exactly one winner."

## вП±пЄП 60-Minute Interview Plan

**Interview mode: B вАФ coding environment. I write and RUN the code; the demo cases + a 23-test suite (incl. a 64-thread race) prove it.**

| Time | What you do | Mode |
|------|-------------|------|
| 0вАУ5 min | **Say ¬І1-2 out loud** вАФ restate "auction", propose scope (one item, highest-bid wins, soft close, outbid notices; cut payments/shipping). | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY |
| 5вАУ8 min | **Pin the API surface (¬І3.5)** вАФ state the 6 public calls: `create_auction`, `start`, `place_bid`, `close`, `highest_bid`, `subscribe`. "Before classes, here's the contract callers use." | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY |
| 8вАУ14 min | **Draw the 5 boxes** вАФ `AuctionService`, `Auction`, `AtomicCell`, `BidValidationStrategy`, `AuctionObserver`. Mention `Bid`/`Bidder`/`AuctionState` verbally. | вЬНпЄП DRAW |
| 14вАУ16 min | **State patterns + the concurrency model** вАФ Optimistic CAS (the highest-bid loop), State (lifecycle), Observer (outbid), Strategy (min-increment); then: "the one race is two bidders raising the top at once вАФ I solve it lock-free with compare-and-set, not a big lock." | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY |
| 16вАУ18 min | **Walk ¬І5.5 approach** вАФ the key insight (CAS on an immutable snapshot), explain `place_bid`'s retry loop in plain words. | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY |
| 18вАУ38 min | **Write code in interview order** (see ¬І6.0): enums вЖТ `AtomicCell` вЖТ `Auction` state machine + CAS loop вЖТ `BidValidationStrategy` вЖТ `AuctionService` skeleton вЖТ `place_bid` вЖТ `close`. | вЬНпЄП WRITE |
| 38вАУ45 min | **Demo Case 1 + Case 5** вАФ bidding war + the 64-thread race. Run `main.py` + `pytest -q`. | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY / run |
| 45вАУ55 min | **Concurrency answer** вАФ CAS vs per-auction lock trade-off; the distributed `UPDATE ... WHERE highest < :amount`. | рЯЧ£пЄП SAY (вЭУ code if asked) |
| 55вАУ60 min | **Follow-up questions** вАФ use the hardest follow-ups from ¬І9 (snipers, ties, 10M auctions). | вЭУ IF ASKED |

**Two interview modes вАФ pick one at the top:**
- **Mode A вАФ Whiteboard / no-run (Amazon, Google):** Skip ¬І7 run output. Demo the bidding war + the race verbally. Write code but don't execute.
- **Mode B вАФ Coding environment (Uber, Meta, startups):** Write and RUN `main.py` + `pytest` in ¬І7. Show actual terminal output. The 64-thread race proves no lost update.

**The one thing that makes interviewers flag you "strong hire":** Proactively say, right after the diagram, "the highest-bid update is a check-then-act race вАФ I'll make it an optimistic compare-and-set loop so a stale bid can never overwrite the real winner." Said early and confidently, that single sentence signals you've built real concurrent systems.

---

## 1. Understanding the Problem & Clarifying Questions

### 1.1 What we're building (plain words)

We're building the engine behind an online auction like eBay: a seller lists an item with a starting price and a deadline, bidders place bids, and the system always tracks the single **current highest bid**. The whole game is that lots of people bid on the *same* item at nearly the same time, so the tricky part is making sure two bids never both "win" вАФ and that a slow, stale bid can't accidentally overwrite a higher one. On top of that, a bid placed in the last few seconds quietly pushes the deadline out (so the auction can't be "sniped"), and whoever just got bumped from the top gets an "you were outbid" ping.

### 1.2 Clarifying questions (candidate-led proposals)

**You:** "For v1 I'll model a single item per auction with one running highest bid вАФ no multi-item lots or quantity. The winner is simply the highest bid when the clock runs out. Sound right?"
**Interviewer:** "Yep, single item, highest bid wins."
*вЬУ scoped: one item, one highest-bid cell per auction вАФ that's the thing every bidder contends on.*

**You:** "The interesting requirement is concurrency: many bidders raise the same top bid at once. I'll guarantee no lost update and exactly one winner, and I'll prove it with a multi-threaded test. That's the core, agreed?"
**Interviewer:** "That's exactly what I want to see."
*good вАФ the concurrency proof is the centerpiece, not a footnote.*

**You:** "I'll do **soft close / anti-snipe**: a bid within the last N seconds extends the deadline by a fixed amount. I'll make N and the extension configurable. OK?"
**Interviewer:** "Yes, include that вАФ it's a common gotcha."
*вЬУ soft close is in scope; deadline lives next to the highest bid so they extend atomically.*

**You:** "Minimum increment policy вАФ I'll make 'how much you must beat the top by' pluggable (flat +X, or +X%). Default flat. That a fair assumption?"
**Interviewer:** "Sure, keep flat as default."
*locks the Strategy seam; if they want tiered increments later it's a new class.*

**You:** "Outbid notifications: I'll fire an event to whoever was just displaced, but actual delivery (email/SMS/push) is out of scope вАФ I just hand the event to an observer. Agreed?"
**Interviewer:** "Agreed, don't build the delivery."
*вЬУ Observer seam in, transport out вАФ we return/emit the event, downstream delivers.*

**You:** "And I'll cut payments, shipping, user auth, and bid retraction for v1 вАФ separate systems. The auction engine ends at 'declare the winner'. OK?"
**Interviewer:** "Good cuts. One thing вАФ make sure a bid after the auction closes is cleanly rejected, not silently dropped."
*OK вАФ every bid reaches a defined outcome: accepted, or a typed rejection (too-low / closed). I'll add `AuctionClosedError`.*

### 1.3 Final Requirements (the whiteboard summary)

**Functional Requirements**

1. **Create an auction** with an item, a starting price, and a duration.
   - Returns an auction id; auction begins in `SCHEDULED`.
   - Rejects non-positive price/duration with a typed error.
2. **Start an auction** (`SCHEDULED вЖТ ACTIVE`), which sets its end time = now + duration.
   - Starting a non-scheduled auction is a typed error.
3. **Place a bid** (`auction_id`, `bidder`, `amount`) on an active auction.
   - Accepted only if it clears the current highest by the minimum increment (or meets the starting price if it's the first bid).
   - Returns the accepted `Bid`; rejects a too-low bid with `BidTooLowError` and a bid on a closed/expired auction with `AuctionClosedError`.
   - Under concurrency: **no lost update** вАФ the highest bid moves monotonically up and exactly one bidder owns it.
4. **Soft close (anti-snipe):** a bid within the last N seconds pushes the end time out by a fixed extension.
5. **Close an auction** (`ACTIVE вЖТ CLOSED`) and return the winning bid (or none).
   - Idempotent вАФ closing twice returns the same winner.
6. **Query the current highest bid** for an auction.
7. **Subscribe** an observer to an auction to receive outbid / new-highest / closed events.

**Out of scope**
- Payment, shipping, fulfillment (downstream systems вАФ we end at "winner declared").
- Bid retraction / sniping bots / proxy (auto) bidding (a v2 strategy).
- Notification *delivery* (we emit the event; transport is downstream).
- User accounts / auth (bidders arrive already identified).

> рЯТђ **What you say to open ¬І2:** "The one thing I want to lock down before modeling is that all the contention is on a single value вАФ the highest bid per auction вАФ so I'll design that as an atomic compare-and-set cell. With that, let me walk through the actors and flows."

## 2. Actors & Core Flows

**Actors**
- **Seller** вАФ creates an auction and starts it; later it closes (by an admin call or a timer at the deadline).
- **Bidder** вАФ places bids; receives outbid / new-highest notifications.
- **Notification channel** (external) вАФ an observer the system emits events to; it owns actual delivery.
- **Close trigger** (system) вАФ whoever calls `close` when the deadline passes (an admin or a background timer).

**Core flows**
1. Seller: `create_auction` вЖТ `start` вЖТ auction is ACTIVE with an end time.
2. Bidder: `place_bid` вЖТ CAS loop validates + swaps the top вЖТ returns accepted bid; displaced bidder gets an OUTBID event.
3. Bidder (late): `place_bid` within the last N seconds вЖТ bid accepted AND the deadline extends (soft close).
4. Bidder (stale/low): `place_bid` below the current top + increment вЖТ `BidTooLowError`, nothing mutated.
5. Close: `close` (at deadline) вЖТ status CLOSED, winner frozen, CLOSED event emitted; any later bid вЖТ `AuctionClosedError`.

## 3. Core Entities (accept / reject)

### 3.1 Candidate noun walkthrough

> **Auction** вАФ The thing being designed: it has an id, an item, a status that moves SCHEDULED вЖТ ACTIVE вЖТ CLOSED, a deadline, and the current highest bid. It owns real state AND behavior (lifecycle transitions, the bid-acceptance rule). *Accepted вАФ it's the state-machine entity at the center.*
>
> **Bid** вАФ A single offer: who made it and how much. It's worth its own type because the highest-bid cell stores it, the increment policy reads its amount, and we compare bids by a version number. Making it an **immutable snapshot** is what lets compare-and-set work by identity. *Accepted вАФ a frozen value object.*
>
> **Bidder** вАФ A participant with an id and a name. They *call* the system; we don't manage their lifecycle вАФ we only remember which bidder owns the current top. *Accepted as a thin value object* (passed into `place_bid`, referenced in events) вАФ borderline actor, but the prompt wants it modeled and it keeps event payloads typed.
>
> **Highest bid / current price** вАФ Tempting to store as a bare `int` field on `Auction`. But that bare field is exactly where the lost-update race lives. I promote it to an **`AtomicCell` holding an immutable `AuctionState`** (highest bid + deadline) so the update is a compare-and-set, not a `+=`. *Accepted вАФ but as a concurrency primitive, not a domain noun.*
>
> **Item** вАФ The thing being sold. Our engine only needs its name for display; the catalog (photos, description, SKU) lives upstream. *Rejected as an entity вАФ modeled as a `str` field on `Auction`.*
>
> **Increment / minimum next bid** вАФ The rule for "how much you must beat the top by". It's behavior that the business will want to tweak (flat vs percent), so it's a **Strategy interface**, not data. *Accepted as `BidValidationStrategy`* (an interface, not an entity-with-state).
>
> **Notification / outbid alert** вАФ A message to a displaced bidder. The *event* is data; *delivering* it is someone else's job. So it's an **Observer seam** plus a small `AuctionEvent` value object. *Accepted as the `AuctionObserver` interface + `AuctionEvent`.*
>
> **Deadline / end time** вАФ A timestamp that soft-close mutates. It must change in the *same atomic step* as the winning bid, so it doesn't live as its own field вАФ it rides inside the `AuctionState` snapshot. *Rejected as a standalone entity вАФ it's a field in the atomically-swapped snapshot.*
>
> **Status (SCHEDULED/ACTIVE/CLOSED)** вАФ A finite set of values with ordering and legal transitions. *Rejected as an entity вАФ it's an enum the `Auction` guards.*
>
> **Seller** вАФ Creates/starts auctions but the system doesn't store seller state in v1. *Rejected вАФ an actor outside the model.*

### 3.2 Final entity table

| Entity | Responsibility | Key fields (typed) | Invariant it guards |
|---|---|---|---|
| `AuctionService` | The orchestrator: owns the registry + observers, mints ids, injects the clock | `_auctions: dict[str, Auction]`, `_observers: dict[str, list[AuctionObserver]]`, `_now: Callable[[], float]` | every public call resolves a known auction or raises `UnknownAuctionError` |
| `Auction` | Lifecycle state machine + the lock-free bid loop + soft close | `id: str`, `status: AuctionStatus`, `_cell: AtomicCell[AuctionState]`, `starting_price: int` | status only moves SCHEDULEDвЖТACTIVEвЖТCLOSED; the highest bid only ever rises |
| `AtomicCell[T]` | A one-slot compare-and-set register | `_value: T`, `_lock: Lock` | a swap commits only if the value is still exactly the snapshot read |
| `Bid` | An immutable snapshot of one bid | `bidder_id: str`, `amount: int`, `version: int` | once created, never mutated (version 0 = the floor) |
| `Bidder` | A participant value object | `id: str`, `name: str` | immutable |
| `AuctionState` | The atomically-swapped snapshot | `highest: Bid`, `ends_at: float` | highest bid and deadline always move together |
| `BidValidationStrategy` | The pluggable min-increment rule (interface) | вАФ | `min_acceptable` returns a strictly-higher-than-current threshold |
| `AuctionObserver` | The notification seam (interface) | вАФ | `notify` never blocks or breaks the bid path |

**Enums**
- `AuctionStatus`: `SCHEDULED`, `ACTIVE`, `CLOSED`
- `EventKind`: `NEW_HIGHEST`, `OUTBID`, `CLOSED`

### 3.3 рЯОЩпЄП Spoken intro

"OK so the model has about eight objects. `AuctionService` is the brain вАФ it owns the auctions, the observer lists, and the clock. `Auction` is the heart: it's a small state machine (SCHEDULED вЖТ ACTIVE вЖТ CLOSED) and it owns the one piece of contended state вАФ the highest bid вАФ which I keep inside an `AtomicCell` so raising it is a compare-and-set, not a plain write. A `Bid` is an immutable snapshot вАФ who and how much, plus a version вАФ and bundling the highest bid with the deadline into an `AuctionState` snapshot is the trick that lets a single CAS update both atomically for soft close. `BidValidationStrategy` is the swappable 'how much must you beat the top by' rule, and `AuctionObserver` is the outbid-notification seam. The pieces I deliberately left out are Item, Seller, the raw deadline, and Status вАФ those are a string field, an actor, a field inside the snapshot, and an enum, respectively."

## 3.5 API / System Interface

The orchestrator's public surface вАФ the only methods external code calls. Everything else (the CAS loop, the soft-close math, the notifications) is internal.

| Method | Signature | Returns | Raises | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `create_auction` | `(item_name: str, starting_price: int, duration: float, min_increment: int = 1, soft_close_window: float = 0.0, extension: float = 0.0, policy: BidValidationStrategy \| None = None) -> str` | the new auction id | `InvalidAuctionError` | Seller lists an item; auction starts SCHEDULED |
| `start` | `(auction_id: str) -> None` | вАФ | `UnknownAuctionError`, `AuctionNotActiveError` | Open the auction for bids; sets end time = now + duration |
| `place_bid` | `(auction_id: str, bidder: Bidder, amount: int) -> Bid` | the accepted bid | `UnknownAuctionError`, `AuctionNotActiveError`, `AuctionClosedError`, `BidTooLowError` | A bidder raises the top via optimistic CAS; soft-close may extend |
| `close` | `(auction_id: str) -> Bid \| None` | the winning bid (or none) | `UnknownAuctionError`, `AuctionNotActiveError` | End the auction, freeze the winner (idempotent) |
| `highest_bid` | `(auction_id: str) -> Bid \| None` | current top bid (or none) | `UnknownAuctionError` | Query the running highest bid |
| `subscribe` | `(auction_id: str, observer: AuctionObserver) -> None` | вАФ | `UnknownAuctionError` | Register an observer for outbid / new-highest / closed events |

If you handed me just this table, you'd know the entire system: bidders only ever call `place_bid`, and every failure mode is a specific typed error, never a silent drop."

## 4. Class Design

### 4.1 Per-class derivation

#### A. `AuctionService` вАФ the orchestrator (first)

**(a) Role.** The system's public API. External code calls it to create, start, bid, close, query, and subscribe. It owns the registry and the observer lists and injects the clock; it never touches an auction's internals directly.

**(b) State derivation**

| Requirement | What this class must remember |
|---|---|
| "Create an auction вА¶ returns an id" | a map `auction_id вЖТ Auction`, plus an id counter |
| "Subscribe an observer to an auction" | a map `auction_id вЖТ list[AuctionObserver]` |
| "Soft close / expiry are testable" | an injected clock `now_fn` |

```
class AuctionService:
    - _auctions: dict[str, Auction]
    - _observers: dict[str, list[AuctionObserver]]
    - _now: Callable[[], float]
    - _ids: counter
```

*State placement trade-off:* the highest bid and deadline are **relational/contended** state, but I do NOT keep them on the service вАФ I push them down into each `Auction`'s `AtomicCell`, because the contention is per-auction. Keeping a single service-wide structure would serialize unrelated auctions; per-auction cells let ten auctions run their CAS loops fully in parallel.

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need from requirements | Method on this class |
|---|---|
| "Create an auction" | `create_auction(...) -> str` |
| "Start / close" | `start(id)`, `close(id) -> Bid \| None` |
| "Place a bid" | `place_bid(id, bidder, amount) -> Bid` |
| "Query highest" | `highest_bid(id) -> Bid \| None` |
| "Subscribe" | `subscribe(id, observer)` |

```
class AuctionService:
    - _auctions: dict[str, Auction]
    - _observers: dict[str, list[AuctionObserver]]
    - _now: Callable[[], float]
    - _ids: counter

    + AuctionService(now_fn = time.monotonic)
    + create_auction(item_name, starting_price, duration, min_increment, soft_close_window, extension, policy) -> str
    + start(auction_id) -> None
    + close(auction_id) -> Bid | None
    + place_bid(auction_id, bidder, amount) -> Bid
    + highest_bid(auction_id) -> Bid | None
    + subscribe(auction_id, observer) -> None
    - _require(auction_id) -> Auction
    - _emit(auction_id, event) -> None
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why does `place_bid` fire notifications, not `Auction`?** The auction owns the *invariant* (the CAS loop); the service owns the *wiring* (who's subscribed). Keeping notifications in the service means the auction has zero dependency on observers, and I can fire events *after* the bid commits, outside any critical section.
- **Why inject `now_fn`?** Soft close and deadline-expiry are time-based; injecting the clock means tests fast-forward instead of sleeping.
- **Why `_require` instead of `dict[id]`?** A raw `dict[id]` raises `KeyError`; `_require` raises the typed `UnknownAuctionError` so callers catch one domain base.

#### B. `Auction` вАФ the lifecycle state machine + bid loop

**(a) Role.** The heart. It guards its own status transitions, runs the optimistic CAS loop to raise its highest bid, and extends its own deadline on a late bid. Only this class may change its status or its highest bid.

**(b) State derivation**

| Requirement | What this class must remember |
|---|---|
| "status moves SCHEDULEDвЖТACTIVEвЖТCLOSED" | `status: AuctionStatus` |
| "track the current highest bid" + "no lost update" | `_cell: AtomicCell[AuctionState]` (highest bid + deadline) |
| "first bid meets starting price" | `starting_price: int` |
| "min increment is pluggable" | `_policy: BidValidationStrategy` |
| "soft close extends the deadline" | `_soft_close_window`, `_extension` |
| "rare lifecycle transitions are safe" | `_lifecycle_lock: Lock` |

```
class Auction:
    - id: str
    - item_name: str
    - status: AuctionStatus
    - starting_price: int
    - _cell: AtomicCell[AuctionState]   # highest bid + deadline, swapped together
    - _policy: BidValidationStrategy
    - _soft_close_window: float
    - _extension: float
    - _now: Callable[[], float]
    - _lifecycle_lock: Lock
```

*State placement trade-off (physical vs relational):* the highest bid is **relational** state managed by the auction, so it lives here вАФ but inside an `AtomicCell` (the concurrency primitive) rather than as a bare field, because a bare field is the lost-update bug. The deadline rides *inside* the same snapshot so soft-close extension and the new bid commit in one CAS.

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need from requirements | Method on this class |
|---|---|
| "start the auction" | `start() -> None` |
| "close + return winner" | `close() -> Bid \| None` |
| "place a bid (CAS loop)" | `place_bid(bidder_id, amount) -> BidOutcome` |
| "query highest" | `highest_bid() -> Bid \| None` |
| "soft-close test reads end time" | `current_ends_at() -> float` |

```
class Auction:
    - id: str
    - item_name: str
    - status: AuctionStatus
    - starting_price: int
    - _cell: AtomicCell[AuctionState]
    - _policy: BidValidationStrategy
    - _soft_close_window: float
    - _extension: float
    - _now: Callable[[], float]
    - _lifecycle_lock: Lock

    + Auction(id, item_name, starting_price, duration, soft_close_window, extension, policy, now_fn)
    + start() -> None
    + close() -> Bid | None
    + place_bid(bidder_id, amount) -> BidOutcome
    + highest_bid() -> Bid | None
    + current_ends_at() -> float
    - _extended_end(ends_at, now) -> float
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why a CAS loop instead of a lock around `place_bid`?** Bidding is the contended hot path. An optimistic loop lets readers and non-conflicting bidders proceed without blocking; only a real conflict (two bids on the same snapshot) costs one retry. It's the same pattern as a database optimistic lock.
- **Why bundle the deadline into the snapshot?** So soft-close extension is atomic with the bid вАФ you can never observe the new bid with the old deadline.
- **Why a separate `_lifecycle_lock`?** `start`/`close` are rare control-plane actions that flip `status`; a tiny mutex makes them mutually exclusive without touching the lock-free bid path.

#### C. `AtomicCell[T]` вАФ the compare-and-set primitive

**(a) Role.** A one-slot register supporting `get` and `compare_and_set`, like Java's `AtomicReference`. The whole concurrency story rests on it.

**(b) State derivation**

| Requirement | What this class must remember |
|---|---|
| "hold the current value" | `_value: T` |
| "make read-check-write indivisible" | `_lock: Lock` (held for nanoseconds) |

```
class AtomicCell~T~:
    - _value: T
    - _lock: Lock
```

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need | Method |
|---|---|
| "read a snapshot" | `get() -> T` |
| "swap only if unchanged" | `compare_and_set(expected, new) -> bool` |

```
class AtomicCell~T~:
    - _value: T
    - _lock: Lock

    + AtomicCell(initial: T)
    + get() -> T
    + compare_and_set(expected: T, new: T) -> bool
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why identity (`is`) and not equality?** Each accepted bid is a brand-new object, so "still the same object I read" means "nobody swapped in between". Reusing values can't happen, so there's no ABA problem.
- **Why a tiny lock if this is 'lock-free'?** The cell uses a lock internally to emulate a hardware CAS instruction; it's held for a single comparison. The *bid logic* (validation, building the new bid) runs outside it вАФ that's what makes the design effectively lock-free under contention.

#### D. `BidValidationStrategy` вАФ the min-increment seam (interface)

**(a) Role.** The pluggable rule for "how much must a new bid beat the current top by". The auction depends on this abstraction, never on a concrete rule.

**(b) State derivation.** None вАФ it's a pure-behavior interface.

```
interface BidValidationStrategy:
    (no state)
```

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need | Method |
|---|---|
| "compute the minimum next bid" | `min_acceptable(current, starting_price) -> int` |

```
interface BidValidationStrategy:
    + min_acceptable(current: Bid, starting_price: int) -> int

class FixedIncrementPolicy implements BidValidationStrategy:
    - _increment: int
    + min_acceptable(current, starting_price) -> int   # floor -> starting_price; else current.amount + increment

class PercentIncrementPolicy implements BidValidationStrategy:
    - _pct: float
    + min_acceptable(current, starting_price) -> int   # floor -> starting_price; else current.amount + ceil(amount*pct)
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why does the floor return the starting price?** The first bid only has to *meet* the start price; every later bid must *beat* the standing top by the increment.
- **Why `ceil` in the percent policy?** A fractional step must round UP to a strictly higher integer, otherwise a tiny percentage could round to +0 and let two bids tie.

#### E. `AuctionObserver` вАФ the notification seam (interface)

**(a) Role.** Decouples "a bid happened" from "tell people about it". The service fires events to observers after a bid commits.

**(b) State derivation.** None for the interface.

```
interface AuctionObserver:
    (no state)
```

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need | Method |
|---|---|
| "notify of outbid / new-highest / closed" | `notify(event: AuctionEvent) -> None` |

```
interface AuctionObserver:
    + notify(event: AuctionEvent) -> None

class ConsoleNotifier implements AuctionObserver:
    + notify(event) -> None        # prints a human line

class RecordingObserver implements AuctionObserver:
    - events: list[AuctionEvent]
    + notify(event) -> None        # appends, for tests/demo
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why an interface, not a hardcoded notifier?** Different deployments want console, queue, or test-recording sinks; new sinks are new classes, zero core change.
- **Why does the service wrap `notify` in try/except?** A broken observer must never break bid delivery or kill the bidder's thread.

#### F. `Bid` вАФ the immutable bid snapshot (value object)

**(a) Role.** A frozen record of one bid, stored inside the highest-bid cell and compared by version.

**(b) State derivation**

| Requirement | What this class must remember |
|---|---|
| "who bid / how much" | `bidder_id: str`, `amount: int` |
| "order bids / CAS by version" | `version: int` (0 = floor) |

```
class Bid (frozen):
    + bidder_id: str
    + amount: int
    + version: int
```

**(c) Operations derivation**

| Need | Method |
|---|---|
| "is this the opening floor?" | `is_floor -> bool` (property) |

```
class Bid (frozen):
    + bidder_id: str
    + amount: int
    + version: int
    + is_floor -> bool        # version == 0
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why frozen?** Immutability is what makes CAS-by-identity safe вАФ a snapshot can never mutate under another thread.
- **Why a version field if CAS uses identity?** The version gives a human-readable monotonic order for the proof (versions 1,2,3,вА¶) and reinforces "no ABA".

#### G. `Bidder` & `AuctionState` (value objects)

**(a) Role.** `Bidder` is a participant (id + name) passed into `place_bid`; `AuctionState` is the atomically-swapped snapshot bundling the highest bid and the deadline.

**(b)/(c) State + operations.** Both are pure data.

```
class Bidder (frozen):
    + id: str
    + name: str

class AuctionState (frozen):
    + highest: Bid
    + ends_at: float
```

**(d) Design choices**
- **Why is `AuctionState` a separate object and not two fields on `Auction`?** Because CAS swaps *one* reference вАФ bundling highest + deadline into one immutable object is what makes "raise the bid AND extend the deadline" a single atomic step.
- **Why model `Bidder` at all if it's almost an actor?** It keeps event payloads and `place_bid` typed (`bidder.id`) instead of passing bare strings around.

**Relationships, one edge at a time:**
- `AuctionService "1" o-- "0..*" Auction` (**aggregation**): the service holds a reference to many auctions in its registry. Open diamond because an `Auction` is a meaningful object on its own вАФ the reference is for lookup/dispatch.
- `Auction "1" *-- "1" AtomicCell` (**composition**): the auction *owns* its cell; kill the auction and the cell goes with it. The cell has no identity outside its auction. This is the contended state, encapsulated.
- `Auction ..> BidValidationStrategy` (**dependency**): the auction *uses* a strategy to compute the min next bid but doesn't own it (it's injected at creation). **This is the extensibility seam** вАФ the auction never names a concrete policy.
- `AuctionService ..> AuctionObserver` (**dependency**): the service calls `notify` on observers after a bid commits; it holds them only to dispatch events.
- `BidValidationStrategy <|.. FixedIncrementPolicy / PercentIncrementPolicy` and `AuctionObserver <|.. ConsoleNotifier / RecordingObserver` (**realization**): the concretes implement the interface contracts.

**Pattern Map**

| Class / Interface | Pattern | One-line reason |
|---|---|---|
| `AtomicCell` + the `place_bid` retry loop | Optimistic Concurrency / Compare-and-Set | raise the top only if it's unchanged; retry on conflict вАФ no lost update |
| `Auction.status` transitions | State | legal lifecycle SCHEDULEDвЖТACTIVEвЖТCLOSED guarded in one place |
| `BidValidationStrategy` | Strategy | the auction depends on the min-increment abstraction, not a concrete |
| `AuctionObserver` | Observer | decouple outbid/closed notifications from the bid path |
| `AuctionState` snapshot | Immutable Snapshot | bundle highest + deadline so one CAS updates both atomically |

рЯОЩпЄП **Script вАФ walk the diagram box-by-box:** "I put `AuctionService` at the top because every call flows through it. Hanging off it is `Auction` вАФ the state machine that owns the contended highest bid. Inside `Auction` is `AtomicCell` вАФ composition, drawn with a filled diamond, because the cell only exists for that auction. On the right is `BidValidationStrategy` вАФ the Strategy seam; the auction asks it for the minimum next bid and never knows if it's flat or percent. `FixedIncrementPolicy` and `PercentIncrementPolicy` implement it. Below, `AuctionObserver` is the notification seam вАФ `ConsoleNotifier` and `RecordingObserver` implement it, and the service fires events after a bid commits. The key seam is `BidValidationStrategy`: new bidding rules plug in as new classes, no core change."

> вЪ†пЄП **PROACTIVE CONCURRENCY FLAG:** "I see the race: `place_bid` reads the current highest, checks the new bid clears it, then writes the new top вАФ that's check-then-act. Two bidders can both read 250, both think they beat it, and one silently overwrites the other вАФ a lost update. Instead of wrapping it in a big lock, I make the write a **compare-and-set**: install my bid only if the top is still exactly what I read; if not, I re-read and re-validate. I'll prove it with a 64-thread barrier test in ¬І9."

## 5. Design Patterns & Principles

### 5.1 Information Expert (stated first)

I followed Information Expert: each class owns the state and behavior closest to its data. `AuctionService` owns the registry and observer lists because it's the only thing that sees all auctions. `Auction` owns its lifecycle and its highest bid because those rules are intrinsic to one auction вАФ so the orchestrator never reaches in and writes a status or a bid directly. `AtomicCell` owns the compare-and-set because making a read-check-write indivisible is its single job. `BidValidationStrategy` owns the increment rule because "how much must you beat the top by" is a pure policy decision with no auction state.

### 5.2 Applied patterns

**1. Optimistic Concurrency Control / Compare-and-Set (the load-bearing one).**
- **Why it fits here:** the contention is a single value (the highest bid) raised by many threads. A pessimistic lock serializes everyone even when bids don't conflict; the natural primitive for "update X only if it hasn't changed" is compare-and-set with a retry loop.
- **Seam:** `AtomicCell.compare_and_set(expected, new)` + the `while True` loop in `Auction.place_bid`.
- рЯОЩпЄП "I treat the highest bid like a database row with optimistic locking: read it, validate against it, and commit only if nobody changed it meanwhile. If they did, I retry вАФ and on retry my bid is usually too low now, so it's cleanly rejected."
- рЯТђ **Layman:** "It's like editing a shared doc: I save my change only if the doc hasn't changed since I opened it. If someone else saved first, I get told and re-check вАФ I never silently clobber their edit."

**2. State (the auction lifecycle).**
- **Why it fits here:** an auction has a strict, ordered lifecycle (SCHEDULED вЖТ ACTIVE вЖТ CLOSED) and the legal action set differs per state (you can't bid before start or after close).
- **Seam:** `Auction.status` + the guards in `start`/`close`/`place_bid` that reject illegal transitions with typed errors.
- рЯОЩпЄП "Only the auction flips its own status, and each transition is guarded, so an illegal jump like bidding on a closed auction is impossible by construction."
- рЯТђ **Layman:** "The auction is like a traffic light with fixed stages вАФ it can't jump from red straight to a stage that doesn't exist."

**3. Observer (outbid notifications).**
- **Why it fits here:** when a bid displaces the top bidder, *someone* must be told, but the bid logic shouldn't care who or how. Observers subscribe; the service fires events after the bid commits.
- **Seam:** `AuctionObserver.notify` + `AuctionService.subscribe` + `_emit`.
- рЯОЩпЄП "The bid path doesn't know about email or push вАФ it commits the bid, then the service hands an event to whoever subscribed."
- рЯТђ **Layman:** "Like a mailing list: the auction announces 'new top bid', and anyone who signed up gets the memo."

**4. Strategy (min-increment) вАФ earned but optional.**
- **Why it fits here:** the increment rule is exactly the thing the business tweaks (flat today, percent for high-value lots tomorrow).
- **Seam:** `BidValidationStrategy.min_acceptable` with two concretes.

### 5.3 SOLID

- **Single Responsibility:** `AtomicCell` owns atomicity, `Auction` owns lifecycle + the bid rule, `AuctionService` owns coordination/notification, `BidValidationStrategy` owns the increment. Four reasons to change, four classes.
- **Open/Closed:** a new bidding rule is a new `BidValidationStrategy`; a new notification sink is a new `AuctionObserver` вАФ zero core edits.
- **Dependency Inversion:** `Auction` depends on the `BidValidationStrategy` abstraction; `AuctionService` depends on the `AuctionObserver` abstraction вАФ never on concretes.
- **Liskov:** any `BidValidationStrategy` returns a strictly-higher threshold, so the auction works with any of them interchangeably.

### 5.4 EARN YOUR PATTERNS

Every claim survives `Cmd-F`: CAS is `AtomicCell.compare_and_set` called in `Auction.place_bid`; State is the `status` guards; Observer is `subscribe`/`_emit`/`notify`; Strategy is `min_acceptable` with two real implementations wired through `create_auction`. I did **not** claim Singleton (the service is just instantiated once by the caller вАФ that's a Facade, not an enforced singleton) and I did **not** call the soft-close a "pattern" вАФ it's just logic inside the CAS step.

## 5.5 Core Algorithms & Approach

### A. The Key Insight

> **Insight:** Make the highest bid an **immutable snapshot** in a **compare-and-set cell**, and raise it with an optimistic retry loop вАФ bundling the deadline into the same snapshot so a bid and its soft-close extension commit atomically.
>
> **Why it works:** "Accept a bid" is fundamentally "read the current top, check I beat it, write the new top" вАФ a check-then-act. If the write is a CAS that succeeds only when the top is still exactly what I read, then two concurrent bids can't both commit on the same snapshot: one wins, the other's CAS fails and it re-reads. On the re-read its amount is now below the new floor, so it's rejected. The highest therefore only ever rises (monotonic), and exactly one bidder owns the final value.
>
> **What breaks without it:** The naive version writes `if amount > top.amount: self.top = new_bid`. Two bidders both read top=250, both pass the check, both write вАФ the higher write can be *overwritten by the lower one* depending on timing. That's a lost update: a real bid of 300 vanishes, the winner is wrong, and money/trust is lost. A single bare field guarded by nothing is the bug; the CAS cell makes it impossible.

### B. Per-Operation Algorithm

#### `place_bid` (the race-prone method) вАФ pseudocode

```
1. if status == SCHEDULED: raise AuctionNotActiveError
2. loop:
3.    state = cell.get()                      # optimistic snapshot (highest + deadline)
4.    now = clock()
5.    if status == CLOSED or now >= state.ends_at: raise AuctionClosedError
6.    floor = policy.min_acceptable(state.highest, starting_price)
7.    if amount < floor: raise BidTooLowError
8.    new_bid = Bid(bidder_id, amount, state.highest.version + 1)
9.    new_end = extended_end(state.ends_at, now)     # soft close
10.   candidate = AuctionState(new_bid, new_end)
11.   if cell.compare_and_set(state, candidate):     # THE atomic step
12.       return BidOutcome(new_bid, displaced, extended?, new_end)
13.   # CAS failed -> someone bid first; loop back to step 3
```

рЯОЩпЄП **Walkthrough (this method has the check-then-act race):** "Steps 3вАУ11 are the critical section, but I don't lock them вАФ I make step 11 a compare-and-set. Step 3 reads the snapshot optimistically. Step 6 is where Strategy fires вАФ the auction asks the policy for the floor. Step 7 is the edge the interviewer probes: a bid equal to the current top is rejected because the floor is top+increment. Step 9 folds the soft-close extension into the *same* candidate, so step 11 installs the bid and the new deadline together. If step 11 fails, another bid landed since step 3 вАФ I loop, re-read, and now step 7 usually rejects me. That retry is exactly why there's no lost update."

#### `place_bid` вАФ DRY RUN

> **Dry run вАФ Bob bids 300; current top is Alice@250 (v3), deadline 1060, now 1055, window 10s, extension 15, increment 10:**
>
> | Step | Action | State change |
> |---|---|---|
> | 3 | `cell.get()` | snapshot S = {highest: Alice@250 v3, ends_at: 1060} |
> | 6 | `policy.min_acceptable(Alice@250, 100)` | floor = 250 + 10 = 260 |
> | 7 | 300 вЙ• 260 вЬУ | continue |
> | 8 | build new bid | Bob@300 v4 |
> | 9 | `extended_end(1060, 1055)` | 1060 вИТ 1055 = 5 вЙ§ 10 вЖТ new_end = 1055 + 15 = **1070** |
> | 11 | `compare_and_set(S, {Bob@300 v4, 1070})` | cell still == S вЖТ **swap succeeds** |
> | 12 | return | `BidOutcome(Bob@300, displaced=Alice, extended=True, 1070)` |
>
> *Edge probe: what if Carol's `compare_and_set` runs between Bob's step 3 and step 11?* *Bob's CAS sees the cell is no longer S (Carol swapped) вЖТ returns False вЖТ Bob loops. On the re-read, if Carol bid 310, Bob's 300 < 320 floor вЖТ `BidTooLowError`. No lost update вАФ Carol's bid stands.*

#### `start` вАФ pseudocode

```
1. lock lifecycle:
2.    if status != SCHEDULED: raise AuctionNotActiveError
3.    install ends_at = now + duration into the cell
4.    status = ACTIVE
```
рЯОЩпЄП "Set the deadline *before* flipping to ACTIVE, so the first bidder who sees ACTIVE already reads a correct end time."

#### `close` вАФ pseudocode

```
1. lock lifecycle:
2.    if status == CLOSED: return current winner      # idempotent
3.    if status == SCHEDULED: raise AuctionNotActiveError
4.    status = CLOSED
5.    return highest_bid()
```
рЯОЩпЄП "Close is idempotent вАФ a timer and an admin can both call it; the second call just returns the same winner."

#### `highest_bid` / `current_ends_at` вАФ lookups

```
highest_bid: h = cell.get().highest; return None if h.is_floor else h
current_ends_at: return cell.get().ends_at
```
рЯОЩпЄП "Both are O(1) reads off the atomic cell вАФ a single `get()`."

#### `min_acceptable` (strategy) вАФ pseudocode

```
if current.is_floor: return starting_price          # first bid meets start price
return current.amount + step                         # flat or ceil(amount*pct)
```

### C. Data Structure Choices

| Data Structure | Used For | Why THIS (vs alternative considered) | Complexity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `AtomicCell[AuctionState]` (one ref) | the highest bid + deadline | a bare `int` field is the lost-update bug; a lock around the whole method serializes non-conflicting bids. CAS on one immutable ref gives correctness with parallelism | O(1) read, O(1) CAS, ~1 retry under conflict |
| immutable `AuctionState` snapshot | bundle highest + deadline | two separate fields can be updated non-atomically (new bid, old deadline). One ref swapped together can't drift | O(1) |
| `dict[str, Auction]` | the registry | sweep/lookup by id is O(1); a list would be O(n) per `place_bid` lookup, and bids are the hot path | O(1) get |
| `dict[str, list[AuctionObserver]]` | per-auction observers | events fan out to one auction's subscribers only; a global list would notify everyone | O(k) emit, k = subscribers |
| `itertools.count` | id minting | numbered ids (`auc-1`) make tests readable; I'd swap to UUID in production | O(1) |

I deliberately chose **one CAS cell over a per-bid lock + a sorted bid book**: I don't need the full history of bids to know the winner вАФ only the running max. If a requirement appeared for "show me all bids in order", I'd add an append-only log *beside* the cell (writes still go through CAS), flipping when audit/history becomes a real requirement.

### D. Tricky Logic

> **рЯІ† Claim:** The CAS must compare by *identity*, and each accepted bid must be a fresh object.
> **рЯФђ Proof:** If two snapshots could be *equal but distinct* (e.g. compare by amount), a thread that read top=250, got displaced to 260, then displaced back to 250 by a cancel, would CAS-succeed against a "250" that isn't the one it read вАФ the ABA problem.
> **рЯЫ†пЄП Fix in code:** `compare_and_set` uses `self._value is expected`, and every accept builds a NEW `Bid`/`AuctionState`, so the same object is never reinstalled. The `version` field makes this auditable.

> **рЯІ† Claim:** Soft-close extension must be atomic with the bid.
> **рЯФђ Proof:** If the bid is CAS'd, then the deadline is written separately, a reader between the two writes sees the new bid with the OLD deadline вАФ and a concurrent close could fire on the stale deadline, dropping a legitimate bid.
> **рЯЫ†пЄП Fix in code:** the deadline lives inside `AuctionState`; one CAS swaps `{new_bid, new_end}` together вАФ there is no in-between state.

> **рЯІ† Claim:** The first bid is "meet", later bids are "beat".
> **рЯФђ Proof:** If the floor always returned `top + increment`, the very first bidder would have to exceed the starting price by the increment вАФ wrong; the start price should be biddable.
> **рЯЫ†пЄП Fix in code:** `min_acceptable` checks `current.is_floor` (version 0) and returns `starting_price` for the first bid.

> **рЯІ† Claim:** `close` must be idempotent.
> **рЯФђ Proof:** A deadline timer and a manual admin close can both fire; the second must not error or change the winner.
> **рЯЫ†пЄП Fix in code:** `close` returns the existing winner if already CLOSED вАФ a no-op second call.

> **рЯІ† Claim:** A bid that loops on CAS must re-check the deadline, not just the amount.
> **рЯФђ Proof:** A long retry could cross the deadline; otherwise a bid that started before close could commit after it.
> **рЯЫ†пЄП Fix in code:** step 5 (the closed/expired check) is *inside* the loop, re-read every retry.

### E. The "what I'd write WRONG first" anti-example

> **вЭМ Naive version a junior would write:**
> ```
> def place_bid(self, bidder_id, amount):
>     if amount > self.highest.amount:        # bare check
>         self.highest = Bid(bidder_id, amount)   # bare write
>         return self.highest
>     raise BidTooLowError(...)
> ```
> **Why it's wrong:** the check and the write are two steps on a bare field. Two bidders both read `highest=250`, both pass `amount > 250`, both write вАФ the 300 bid can be overwritten by a 260 bid depending on scheduling. That's a **lost update**: the real winner disappears. The correct version reads an immutable snapshot, validates against it, and commits with `compare_and_set` so only one writer wins per snapshot.

class AuctionStatus(Enum):
    SCHEDULED = "SCHEDULED"  # created, not yet open for bids
    ACTIVE = "ACTIVE"        # open вАФ bids accepted
    CLOSED = "CLOSED"        # ended вАФ winner frozen, no more bids


class EventKind(Enum):
    NEW_HIGHEST = "NEW_HIGHEST"
    OUTBID = "OUTBID"
    CLOSED = "CLOSED"


class AuctionError(Exception):
    """Base for every error this system raises вАФ callers catch one type."""

class UnknownAuctionError(AuctionError): ...
class InvalidAuctionError(AuctionError): ...
class AuctionNotActiveError(AuctionError): ...
class AuctionClosedError(AuctionError): ...
class BidTooLowError(AuctionError): ...

### `AtomicCell` вАФ the compare-and-set primitive

**Role.** A one-slot register that makes read-check-write a single indivisible step.

```python copy
class AtomicCell(Generic[T]):
    def __init__(self, initial: T) -> None:
        self._value = initial
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def get(self) -> T:
        with self._lock:
            return self._value

    def compare_and_set(self, expected: T, new: T) -> bool:
        # WHY: identity check (`is`), not equality. Each accepted bid is a NEW
        #      object, so "still the same object I read" means "nobody swapped in
        #      between". That defeats the ABA problem by construction.
        with self._lock:
            if self._value is expected:
                self._value = new
                return True
            return False
```

рЯОЩпЄП "It's a software stand-in for a hardware CAS: swap only if the value hasn't changed since I looked." вЪ†пЄП "Isn't a lock here cheating on 'lock-free'?" вЖТ "The lock is held for one comparison вАФ nanoseconds. The bid *logic* runs outside it, so under contention bidders don't block each other; that's the win."
